title: ap_context() rebuilds static JSON-LD context tree on every call
description: activitypub.rs:29 ap_context() constructs a complex serde_json::Value containing ~20 string allocations, nested maps, and arrays. This is called on every actor document request (GET /users/{username}). The value is always identical—it should be a static LazyLock<Value> allocated once. What goes wrong: every actor fetch (which remote servers hit frequently during federation) allocates and drops a non-trivial JSON tree for data that never changes.
